item.title: Trump to impose 'forced labor' duties on Friday as temporary 10% US tariffs expire
item.description:
By David Lawder

July 23 (Reuters) - The Trump administration on Friday will impose new tariffs of 10% and 12.5% on goods from 60 trading partners, including the European Union, over allegations of lax enforcement of forced labor bans, just as a temporary 10% global tariff expires.

item.title: Iran ramps up executions under shadow of war
item.description:
Vahid Baniamerian's family only found out he was going to be executed when Iranian state media reports flashed on their phones, saying the supreme court would review his death sentence.

"They did not give any warning. Even the lawyer didn't know," a close relative of Baniamerian, based outside of Iran, told AFP, asking not to be identified.

"It was completely illegal, even based on their own oppressive laws."

item.title: UN rights chief Volker Turk faces opposition in vote on new term
item.description:
By Emma Farge, Olivia Le Poidevin and David Brunnstrom

GENEVA, July 23 (Reuters) - United Nations Human Rights chief Volker Turk, an outspoken critic of Russia's war in Ukraine and Israel's conduct in Gaza, faces opposition from both countries to a second four-year term ahead of a potentially contentious vote on Friday, diplomats told Reuters.

Turk, a lawyer who has worked for the United Nations for decades in major offices such as Geneva and field locations includingKosovo, ends his first four-year term in October.

item.title: US House votes to curb Trump's Iran war powers but Senate backs president
item.description:
By Patricia Zengerle

WASHINGTON, July 23 (Reuters) - The Republican-led U.S. House of Representatives backed a resolution on Thursday directing President Donald Trump to halt U.S. military action against Iran unless he obtains Congress' approval, but the Senate voted hours later to block a separate, similar measure.

The House vote, the latest rebuke of Trump from Congress, was 214-208 in favor of the war powers resolution as four Republicans joined Democrats in voting for it.

item.title: UK says armed forces ready to defend country after Iran warning over US bombers
item.description:
LONDON, July 23 (Reuters) - Britain said on Thursday its armed forces were ready to protect the country from any attack after Iran's Revolutionary Guards issued a warning over allowing U.S. bombers to fly from UK bases.

"Our Armed Forces are ready to keep the United Kingdom safe from any kind of attacks, whether it's on our soil or from abroad. The UK stands ready 24/7 to defend itself," a government spokesperson said.

item.title: Top ally of Turkey’s Erdogan backs state funding for Ozel’s breakaway party
item.description: Devlet Bahceli’s suggestion exposes a more nuanced position within the country’s governing alliance over the ongoing legal crackdown against Turkey’s main opposition.

item.title: Top ally of Turkey’s Erdogan backs state funding for Ozel’s breakaway party
item.description: Devlet Bahceli’s suggestion exposes a more nuanced position within the country’s governing alliance over the ongoing legal crackdown against Turkey’s main opposition.

item.title: Top ally of Turkey’s Erdogan backs state funding for Ozel’s breakaway party
item.description: Devlet Bahceli’s suggestion exposes a more nuanced position within the country’s governing alliance over the ongoing legal crackdown against Turkey’s main opposition.

item.title: Top ally of Turkey’s Erdogan backs state funding for Ozel’s breakaway party
item.description: Devlet Bahceli’s suggestion exposes a more nuanced position within the country’s governing alliance over the ongoing legal crackdown against Turkey’s main opposition.

item.title: Bab al-Mandeb Strait: another key oil shipping route under threat
item.description:
Yemen's pro-Iranian Houthi rebels have claimed attacks in the Red Sea on two Saudi oil tankers and say they will blockade the Bab al-Mandeb strait, a crucial passage into the Red Sea, as part of a maritime blockade of Saudi Arabia.

- Gateway to the Red Sea -

Known as the "Gate of Tears" in Arabic, the Bab al-Mandeb strait sits at the southern tip of the Red Sea, connecting it with the Gulf of Aden in the Indian Ocean.

item.title: US Senate blocks resolution curbing Trump's Iran war powers
item.description:
WASHINGTON, July 23 (Reuters) - The U.S. Senate on Thursday blocked a Democratic-led resolution aiming to stop the Iran war until hostilities are authorized by Congress. The resolution reflects growing congressional concern, even among President Donald Trump's Republicans, over the war.

The Senate voted 49 to 47 not to advance the war powers resolution.

(Reporting by Patricia Zengerle; Editing by Don Durfee)

item.title: US House passes symbolic Iran war rebuke to Trump
item.description:
The US House of Representatives passed a largely symbolic resolution Thursday calling for President Donald Trump to halt military action against Iran, delivering another congressional rebuke as the Middle East war sharply escalates.

The measure -- which passed 214-208 after four Republicans joined opposition Democrats in support -- directs Trump to remove US forces from hostilities against Iran unless Congress explicitly authorizes the war.

item.title: Trump stuns Saudis by pinning nuclear deal to Israel ties
item.description:
US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel, throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed.

The apparently new condition comes despite Riyadh having long resisted joining the so-called Abraham Accords with Israel, insisting on a pathway to a Palestinian state.

It was not mentioned in the civilian nuclear deal announced on Wednesday, which had appeared to be a major win for the Saudis even as Trump wages war on Iran over its nuclear program.

item.title: US House votes to halt Iran war, in another symbolic rebuke of Trump
item.description:
By Patricia Zengerle

WASHINGTON, July 23 (Reuters) - The Republican-led U.S. House of Representatives backed legislation on Thursday directing President Donald Trump to halt U.S. military action against Iran, the latest symbolic rebuke of the Republican president from Congress.

The vote was 214-208 in favor of the war powers resolution as four Republicans joined Democrats in voting for it.

item.title: Concerns of Palestinian economic crisis grow as Israeli banks prepare to cut ties
item.description:
By Steven Scheer and Ali Sawafta

JERUSALEM/RAMALLAH, July 23 (Reuters) - Two Israeli banks will halt working with their Palestinian counterparts in the coming months citing risks of associating with possible terrorism financing and money laundering, Israeli officials said, fuelling concerns of a Palestinian economic crisis.

item.title: US vows to punish Iran after Houthis strike tankers
Syria's new leaders seek investment, but at what cost?
item.description:
From new neighbourhoods and commercial towers to leisure developments, Syria's authorities are seeking to attract foreign investors through a sovereign wealth fund created by President Ahmed al-Sharaa that critics say lacks transparency.

The ambitious development proposals even include the former army headquarters in central Damascus, prime real estate left abandoned after an Israeli strike damaged it last year.

After 13 years of civil war and decades of sanctions, the country is in dire need of reconstruction, but money to do so is scarce.

item.title: As Turkey rivalry heats up, Greece to acquire $4B Israeli air defense system
item.description: The Greek defense minister said that the air defense system will be operational within 35 months.

item.title: As Turkey rivalry heats up, Greece to acquire $4B Israeli air defense system
item.description: The Greek defense minister said that the air defense system will be operational within 35 months.
